data.title: "Brother Machi" has accumulated losses of $15.73 million over the past 35 days, and his current long position with a leverage of 25 times is showing a floating loss of $2.27 million.
data.description: Crypto analyst Ai (@ai_9684xtpa) monitors data showing that in the 35 days since the market crash on October 11th, Machi made 18 trades on Hyperliquid, with 5 profitable trades and 13 losing trades, a win rate of 27.7%, all of which were long positions. During this period, her cumulative losses reached $15.73 million, and she added $6.102 million in margin. Currently, Machi's account has approximately $946,000 in remaining margin, holding 7,500.7 ETH long positions with 25x leverage, a position size of approximately $23.68 million, and a current unrealized loss of approximately $2.27 million. The liquidation price is $3,043.16.

data.description:

source: https://www.theblock.co/post/378853/spot-bitcoin-etfs-see-second-largest-outflows?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Machines Face Shutdown Price Challenges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Antpool indicates that several Bitcoin mining machines, including the Antminer S19 series and models below the Whatsminer M60 series, such as the Whatsminer M53S++ and M56S++, have reached their shutdown price. This calculation is based on the current Bitcoin mining difficulty and an electricity cost of $0.08 per kilowatt-hour.

The Whatsminer M60 and M66 models are nearing a shutdown price of $93,000. In contrast, high-performance mining machines like the Antminer U3S23H and Antminer S23 Hyd have a shutdown price above $44,000.

data.title: Antminer S19 and other models below Shenma M60 have reached the shutdown price.
data.description: According to Antpool data, under current Bitcoin mining difficulty and an electricity cost of $0.08 per kilowatt-hour, the Antminer S19 series and Whatsminer models below the M60 (including M53S++ and M56S++) have reached their shutdown price; while the shutdown price of the Whatsminer M60 and M66 series is close to $93,000. Meanwhile, high-hashrate models such as the Antminer U3S23H and Antminer S23 Hyd have shutdown prices above $44,000.

data.title: Bitcoin Investors' Average Cost Suggests Uncertain Bear Market Cycle
data.description: According to Odaily, Ki Young Ju, founder and CEO of CryptoQuant, shared insights on the X platform regarding Bitcoin investments. He noted that the average cost for Bitcoin investors who entered the market in the past 6 to 12 months is approximately $94,000. Ju emphasized that a bear market cycle can only be confirmed if prices fall below this cost range. He advised against drawing premature conclusions and suggested maintaining a cautious stance.

data.title: Bitwise CEO: The crypto market may have been in a bear market for nearly six months and is nearing its end.
data.description: Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley stated in an article on the X platform that the traditional "four-year crypto cycle" model is no longer applicable to the current market structure. Since the launch of the Bitcoin ETF and changes in the regulatory environment, market participants and trading motivations have undergone significant shifts. He noted that while the crypto market may have been in a bear market for the past six months, it is now nearing the end of the downturn and believes that the overall allocation environment for crypto assets has "never been stronger."

data.title: CryptoQuant CEO: Bitcoin may only confirm a bear market if it falls below the 94K cost zone.
data.description: Ki Young Ju, founder and CEO of CryptoQuant, stated in an article on the X platform that the average cost for Bitcoin investors who entered the market over the past 6 to 12 months is around $94,000. He said that a bear market cycle can only be confirmed when the price falls below this cost range, and it is not advisable to draw conclusions prematurely at present; it is better to remain on the sidelines and wait and see.

data.title: Pieverse (PIEVERSE) will soon be available on the Bybit spot trading platform.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Bybit will list Pieverse (PIEVERSE) on the spot market on November 14th. Deposits will be open at 3:00 AM (UTC) on November 14th. Trading will begin at 1:00 PM (UTC) on November 14th. Withdrawals will begin at 10:00 AM (UTC) on November 15th. Pieverse is a compliant payment infrastructure for agent-natives. Its core mission is to build a Web3-based payment network that enables verifiable value recording by providing on-chain timestamping authentication for invoices, receipts, and checks.

data.title: The "ETH shorting whale" that previously borrowed cryptocurrency added another 16,937 ETH to its holdings, bringing its total holdings to 422,175 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, the address that previously borrowed to short ETH has just bought another 16,937 ETH, worth approximately $53.91 million. This address has now bought a total of 422,175 ETH, with a total transaction value of approximately $1.34 billion.

data.title: Physicists Discover “Quantum Mirrors” That Trap Light in 2D Materials
data.description:
Chip-scale terahertz spectroscopy revealed that 2D materials naturally trap light in “quantum mirrors,” opening new ways to study and manipulate exotic quantum behaviors.
source: https://catenaa.com/industries/technology/physicists-discover-quantum-mirrors-that-trap-light-in-2d-materials/

data.title: Bitcoin Crashes To $98,000 As HODLer Selling Accelerates
data.description:
On-chain data shows Bitcoin long-term holders have been ramping up their selling recently, a potential reason behind BTC’s fall under $100,000. Bitcoin Long-Term Holders Have Been Booking Profits In a new post on X, on-chain analytics firm Glassnode has discussed about the latest trend in the supply of the Bitcoin long-term holders (LTHs). These are referred to as the investors holding their coins for a period longer than 155 days, without selling or involving them in a transaction on the blockchain. Related Reading: Bitcoin Sentiment Most Fearful Since March: Is A Bottom Near? Statistically, the longer an investor holds onto their coins, the less likely they become to sell them in the future
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-selling-accelerates-btc-holds-100000/

data.title: US regulator mulls guidance for tokenized deposit insurance, stablecoins
data.description:
Acting FDIC Chair Travis Hill said the agency is also working on a regime for stablecoin issuance and expects to issue a proposal for an application process by the end of year.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/fdic-tokenized-deposit-insurance-stablecoin-application-plans?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss%3Fvfff%3D1763099951%26r%3Dejdwuz&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Orama Labs launches a $5 million ecosystem fund; Kingnet AI and ZENO receive the first batch of grants.
data.description: Orama Labs, a DeSci & AI asset protocol, announced the establishment of a $5 million ecosystem fund to support the development of cutting-edge technology projects. The first batch of grants has been officially distributed, with Kingnet AI receiving $1 million in $PYTHIA tokens and spatial intelligence project ZENO receiving $200,000 in $PYTHIA tokens. Notably, the ZENO project is driving the transition of "spatial intelligence" from theory to practice, and its mirror world platform echoes the industry prediction made by Stanford AI guru Fei-Fei Li that "the next big thing in AI is spatial intelligence." Kingnet AI, as an AI-driven game creation engine, significantly reduces game development costs through natural language processing technology; this award will deepen the cooperation between the two parties at the technology and ecosystem levels. The Orama ecosystem fund will continue to select projects with innovative technologies and clear business models, providing funding, resources, and full-chain support, with a focus on cutting-edge fields such as DeSci, AI, and DEPIN. [Source Link]

data.title: Luxembourg Sovereign Wealth Fund Chose Only Bitcoin, ‘There’s No Second Best’: Finance Minister
data.description:

source: https://cryptonews.com/news/luxembourg-sovereign-wealth-fund-choose-bitcoin/
